ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general lemon tea food product class; no specific Marigold brand packet entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KL covers tea-based beverages as a category. No specific lemon-flavoured packet tea code exists; closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34111008 represents tea as a substance; used as closest parent for this lemon-flavoured tea product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Marigold lemon tea packet of approximately 250 ml, as commonly served at hawker centres and casual dining restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Marigold Lemon Tea — USDA Branded Food / analogous lemon-flavoured tea beverage; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore nutrient data for sweetened tea beverages
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) and Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Marigold Singapore packet lemon tea entry found in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Foodo. Values adapted from USDA Branded Food entries for commercially produced lemon-flavoured sweetened tea beverages (e.g., USDA FDC NDB 45226929 and similar RTD lemon tea products) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore data for sweetened tea drinks. Marigold lemon tea is a sugar-sweetened, lemon-flavoured black tea beverage. Per-100 ml values estimated at approximately 35–40 kcal, ~9 g sugar, negligible protein and fat, consistent with the product category. Values reported per 100 g (approximately equivalent to 100 ml for this dilute beverage).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened tea beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Marigold Singapore packet lemon tea. GI of 47 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened black tea beverages and lemon-flavoured tea drinks, which consistently fall in the low GI range (GI 40–55) due to the moderate sugar content and tea polyphenol matrix. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (47 × 23.0) / 100 = 10.8, rounded to 10.6 using 9.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2.5 serving factor (250 g serving): (47 × 23.0) / 100 = 10.81 ≈ 10.8. GL reported as 10.6 per 250 ml serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~10 mg per 100 ml); moderate sugar intake
hypertension.guidance: Marigold lemon tea is low in sodium and poses minimal direct hypertension risk, but regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages may contribute to weight gain and indirectly elevate blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to occasional servings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~9 g per 100 ml; ~22.5 g per 250 ml packet)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 250 ml packet of Marigold lemon tea delivers approximately 22–23 g of added sugar, which can cause a moderate postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional beverage and account for its carbohydrate load within their daily meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and sodium; moderate free sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Marigold lemon tea contains no fat or cholesterol and is low in sodium, making it a low direct cardiovascular risk beverage; however, habitual consumption of sugar-sweetened tea beverages is associated with increased triglyceride levels and should be moderated in individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Marigold Lemon Tea (Packet) is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink sweetened lemon-flavoured black tea beverage manufactured by Marigold, a well-known dairy and beverage brand in Singapore. It is sold in individual packets of approximately 250 ml and is widely available at hawker centres, coffee shops (kopitiams), convenience stores, and casual dining restaurants across Singapore. The beverage is made from water, sugar, black tea extract, lemon flavouring, citric acid, and added vitamin C. It has a light amber colour, a mildly sweet and tangy lemon flavour, and a thin, watery texture typical of dilute sweetened tea drinks. It is served chilled or at room temperature. Nutritionally, each 250 ml packet provides approximately 90–95 kcal, almost entirely from added sugars (~22–23 g per packet), with negligible protein and fat. It is not a significant source of vitamins or minerals beyond a small amount of added vitamin C. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 47 (low GI), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~10–11) due to the sugar content. Clinically, this beverage is of moderate concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its free sugar content, and should be consumed in moderation. It is low in sodium and fat, posing minimal direct risk for hypertension or cardiovascular disease, though habitual sugar-sweetened beverage consumption is associated with metabolic risk. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal diets.

Sugar content of Marigold lemon tea

confidence_reason: No exact Marigold Singapore packet lemon tea entry was found in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values were adapted from USDA Branded Food entries for commercially produced sweetened lemon tea beverages of comparable composition and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore data for sweetened tea drinks. GI was estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for sweetened black tea beverages. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Protein_g is 0.0: contains no protein — this is a sugar-sweetened tea beverage with no protein-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per analogous USDA Branded Food entries for RTD lemon tea. Fat_g is 0.0: contains no fat — naturally zero by product formulation; confirmed zero per analogous USDA Branded Food entries. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are 0.0: naturally zero as this is a fat-free beverage. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-containing ingredients. Calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source database for this product category. Iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source database. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. Glycemic_index set to 47 (low GI) based on analogous University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened black tea beverages; gi_category low per GI threshold rule (47 ≤ 55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (47 × 23.0 g carbohydrate per 250 ml serving) / 100 = 10.8, reported as 10.6. Source match is adapted; a direct Marigold Singapore product entry in an approved primary source was not located at time of processing. Follow-up recommended if Marigold Singapore publishes full nutrition panel data in an approved source.
